item.title: Visiting Bruges
item.description: Bruges, Ostend and Brussels have been added to the travel pages on Europe. You can read more about it here: http://pgoh13.com/bruges.html

item.title: What's new in Paris
item.description: The latest attraction in Paris is La Canopee at the Forum des Halles which is now open to the public after nearly 6 years of restructuration work. You can read more about it here: http://pgoh13.com/paris_intro.php

item.title: Today's World
item.description:
In "Today's World":
(1) Brexit: David Cameron boasts of UK having "the best of both worlds" by remaining in the EU after obtaining "special status" on migrant benefits for 7 years
(2) Police union calls for law enforcement labor to boycott Beyonce's world tour. "To taint police officers globally in the Super Bowl is really wrong," says official.
See http://pgoh13.com

item.title: Songs of Today, Songs of Yesterday
item.description: The new website called "Songs of Today, Songs of Yesterday" created with smartphones in mind has been completed. It replaces "Songs and Lyrics". Check it out: http://pgoh13.com/mobile/

item.title: Songs And Lyrics - a website for those who like to sing with the lyrics.
item.description: These songs (together with their lyrics) have been added: History (One Direction), Fast Car (Jonas Blue ft. Dakota), Middle (DJ Snake ft. Bipolar Sunshine), Forever Young (Louisa Johnson), Shut Up (Stormzy), Sweet Lovin' (Sigala ft. Bryn Christopher), Same Old Love (Selena Gomez), I'll Show You (Justin Bieber), Take Me Home (Jess Glynne), Over And Over Again (Nathan Sykes), Here (Alessia Cara), The Trouble With Us (Marcus Marr and Chet Faker), Ocean Drive (Duke Dumont), Tennessee Whiskey (Chris Stapleton), Adventure Of A Lifetime (Coldplay), Love Yourself (Justin Bieber), Ex's & Oh's (Elle King), Sax (Fleur East), In2 (WSTRN), Focus (Ariana Grande), Straight Outta Compton (N.W.A.), Never Forget You (Zara Larsson, MNEK), Hello (Adele), Sorry (Justin Bieber), Turn The Music Louder Rumble (KDA ft. Tinie Tempah and Katy B), Perfect (One Direction), Be Right There (Diplo and Sleepy Tom), Wicked Game (Cyrus Villanueva), Lay It All On Me (Rudimental ft. Ed Sheeran), Dessert (Dawin), Wasn't Expecting That (Jamie Lawson), Alone No More (Philip George and Anton Powers), Stitches (Shawn Mendes), This Ain't Love (Jessica Mauboy), Alive (Sia), Runnin' Lose It All (Naughty Boy ft. Beyonc?, Arrow Benjamin), Sugar (Robin Schulz ft. Francesco Yates), Writing's On The Wall (Sam Smith), Hula Hoop (Omi), On My Mind (Ellie Goulding), Hotline Bling (Drake), Cheyenne (Jason Derulo), All Eyes On You (Meek Mill ft. Nicki Minaj and Chris Brown).

item.title: QR (Quick Response) code for The World At Your Fingertips website
item.description:
This is the QR (Quick Response) code for The World At Your Fingertips website. Use your smartphone to scan it and you will be able to access the site just as easily as you would with an Android app.

But in order to scan it (if you don't already have a QR code scanner app) you will first have to download such an app. Among them is QR Droid code scanner that you can download from here: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=la.droid.qr (it's free).
And if you are lucky enough you might be able to find a QR code at your regular bus-stop for you to scan so you will know when the next bus will arrive, even when you are at home preparing to go there!

item.title: Alice Olivia's cover of Writing's On The Wall
item.description: The top song for today is Writing's On The Wall. It is not Sam Smith's version which is in the charts but not available in YouTube because of copyright claims. I am featuring Alice Olivia's cover of the same song. Time to give the lesser known singers, who don't have the advantage of vast media exposure, a hearing!

item.title: Earlier videos/news items from Today's World
item.description: Since September 20, 2015 the home page has a daily top video/news item reflecting different aspects of life in the world of which we are a part. You can see some of the earlier videos/news items here: http://pgoh13.com/index_topvideos.php

item.title: Using Hola! to access blocked websites
item.description: Hola! again came to my rescue tonight. I had wanted to watch the two tries by Mike Brown in England's victory over Fiji on the opening night of the Rugby World Cup opener in England when I came across the following message: "This content is not available in your country" (I'm surfing from France). So I got around this by clicking on my Hola! icon and under "Select a country" I clicked on United Kingdom. And it worked! I could now watch the clip because it is as if I'm now surfing from the U.K.

item.title: Visiting Krakow in Poland
item.description:
Krakow is one of the cheaper cities to visit in Europe. As a matter of fact it took fourth place (after Kiev, Bucharest and Sofia) among 56 European cities compared in a survey of the most affordable places to visit in Europe in 2015. Many tips and photos in this latest addition to the "Visiting Europe" series.
Keith Paterson, MBE writes about his trip to Riga and has many great photos here: http://pgoh13.com/riga.php
The following touristy cities in Europe have already been covered: Berlin, Milan, Lisbon, Dublin, Sicily, Amsterdam, Athens, Barcelona, Cannes, Copenhagen, Geneva, London, Madrid, Paris, Prague, Rome, Venice, Budapest, Sevilla etc.

item.title: Brainteasers that test your ability to "think out of the box"
item.description:
This Mensa brainteaser has been added:
Prior to a sale 17 tee shirts sold at £8 each.
The Summer sale began and a further 85 tee shirts were sold at a reduced price.
The average amount paid for each tee shirt over the whole time was £6.
What was the sale price?
If you have not heard of the "Cheryl's Birthday Puzzle" that went viral recently here it is. This is just another exercise in logical and analytical thinking. If you are an expert in Sudoku puzzles you will be familiar with the idea of using given clues to eliminate likely candidates in order to get closer to the solution, which is quite the case here.

item.title: Using Paragon Backup and Recovery to create a system image
item.description: It has happened to me and it has happened to many other people. You wake up one morning to find that your computer just refuses to start and you have no other alternative but to stare at a blank screen that shows no sign of life. What then? It looks like it's going to be the end of the world. But it needn't be. Yes it needn't be such a tragedy if you had taken the trouble to backup not only your personal files but also your computer settings. Learn how to do it with Paragon Backup and Recovery here.

item.title: Using PIXLR to superimpose one photo on another
item.description: In many group photos there is always one person missing - the one who took the photo! What a pity and how unjust it is for him! Here is a step-by-step explanation on how to use PIXLR to superimpose the photographer into the group photo. http://pgoh13.com/using_pixlr_masked.php

item.title: Using Print Edit to remove unwanted stuff before printing
item.description: You are faced with having to print lots of unwanted stuff (photos, videos, advertisements, sidebars, banners etc.) when you want to print something from internet. They not only do not serve any purpose but dry up your ink as well. The solution is to use Print Edit, which is not only very effective but also free to use. Here's how you can make use of it, with plenty of screenshots for illustration: http://pgoh13.com/using_printedit.php

item.title: New "slim" look for smartphone users
item.description:
This page has been removed after trying it out for over a month as smartphones today make it so easy to zoom in and out, so despite the smaller print due to the size of the page users would still have no difficulty zooming in the part that they are interested in.
(earlier RSS dated 26 May 2015) Regular visitors must have noticed the new "slim" look in the opening page of the website recently. This is actually an effort to create a mobile version of The World At Your Fingertips home page in keeping with the ubiquitous use of smartphones all over the world to access the net. Unfortunately the website is too big for a complete change as its 2,000 over pages have been designed for desktop computers. Sorry about that.

item.title: Lucky or unlucky? You tell me!
item.description: Lucky or unlucky? You tell me! Two stories to set you thinking about the meaning of life here: http://pgoh13.com/lucky_unlucky.php

item.title: Using ManageFlitter to "unfollow" people you never asked to follow in Twitter
item.description: If you are "following" hundreds of people whom you have never asked to follow in Twitter because you are a victim of some automated app or program, don't worry unnecessarily. You can easily "unfollow" them by using ManageFlitter. Here's how to use ManageFlitter.

item.title: Songs And Lyrics - a website for those who like to sing with the lyrics.
item.description: These songs (together with their lyrics) have been added: Like I'm Gonna Lose You (Meghan Trainor ft. John Legend), Start Again (Conrad Sewell), Headlights (Robin Schulz ft. Ilsey), You Don't Own Me (Grace ft. G-Eazy), Powerful (Major Lazer ft. Ellie Goulding & Tarrus Riley), House Every Weekend (David Zowie), Worth It (Fifth Harmony ft. Kid Ink), Wings (Birdy), Animals (Maroon 5), Fight Song (Rachel Platten), Glitterball (Sigma ft. Ella Henderson), Freak Of The Week (Krept & Konan ft. Jeremih). They are among the Top 10 hits in the U.S., U.K., Australian and Canadian charts.

item.title: Learn Indonesian with Malay
item.description: A new list of common Indonesian words that are quite different from Malay has been added as well as more sentences for translation from English to Indonesian. See: http://pgoh13.com/mycourse/indon_tables.php

item.title: Songs And Lyrics - a website for those who like to sing with the lyrics.
item.description: The following songs (together with their lyrics) have been added: Not Letting Go (Tinie Tempah ft. Jess Glynne), Black Magic (Little Mix), Flashlight (Jessie J), Honey, I'm Good (Andy Grammer), Nasty Freestyle (T-Wayne), Don't Look Down (Martin Garrix ft. Usher), Five More Hours (Deorro & Chris Brown), Together (Ella Eyre), Runaway U & I (Galantis), Where Are U Now (Skrillex and Diplo ft. Justin Bieber), Hey Mama (David Guetta ft. Nicki Minaj, Bebe Rexha & Afrojack), Pretty Girls (Britney Spears ft. Iggy Azalea), Photograph (Ed Sheeran), Bad Blood (Taylor Swift ft. Kendrick Lamar)

item.title: ALL GONE! Love locks = broken hearts
item.description:
Where public security is in danger there is no place for sentimentalism. All the "love locks" at the Pont des Arts bridge near to the Louvre-Rivoli subway have been removed under instructions from Paris City Hall who fear that the total weight of the padlocks will cause the bridge to collapse.
The next "padlocks removal operation" will be at the Pont de l'Archeveche bridge (see photo in website) near Notre-Dame. So if you are one of the few romantic couples who have kept the key to the lock (most throw them into the Seine River after locking them) make a quick trip to Paris if you want to recover it for sentimental reasons!

item.title: One place for privacy settings of Gmail, YouTube and Google+
item.description:
Do you know that from June 1, 2015 there is just one place to go to if you want to check on your privacy settings for every Google-owned account (such as Gmail, YouTube, Google+). Once you are logged into anyone of those accounts you will be able to see the settings that affect all the other Google-owned accounts as well. This is the url of the page to go to: https://myaccount.google.com
It is also from here that you can tell Google NOT to keep track of your surfing in order to display ads that reflect your surfing history.

item.title: Songs And Lyrics - a website for those who like to sing with the lyrics.
item.description: The following songs (together with their lyrics) have been added: Lean On (Major Lazer & DJ Snake ft. MO), All Cried Out (Blonde ft. Alex Newell), American Oxygen (Rihanna), Bills (LunchMoney Lewis), Homeless (Marina Kaye), One Last Time (Ariana Grande), Big Girls Cry (Sia), Shut Up and Dance (Walk The Moon), She Looks So Perfect (5 Seconds Of Summer), Marvin Gaye (Charlie Puth ft. Meghan Trainor), See You Again (Wiz Khalifa ft. Charlie Puth), Cheerleader (OMI), Impossible (James Arthur), Bloodstream (Ed Sheeran), Want To Want Me (Jason Derulo), Seasons (Olly Murs), Rumors (Pep & Rash), This World (Selah Sue), West Coast (Lana Del Rey), Trap Queen (Fetty Wap), Wish You Were Mine (Philip George), Higher (Sigma ft. Labrinth), Mirror Man (Ella Henderson) and Hold My Hand (Jess Glynne)

item.title: List of the 1,000 English songs (evergreens, oldies and popular current hits) in this site.
item.description: Here is a list of the 1,000 English songs (evergreens, oldies and popular current hits) with their YouTube clips and lyrics that you will find in this site, grouped together in alphabetical order for easy reference.

item.title: How to use Partition Wizard to create a new drive
item.description: If your new PC or your present one has only one main drive (C:) you might want to consider creating another drive to put all your personal files there so that in case your PC breaks down and Drive C: has to be reformatted in order to reinstall Windows (or any other operating system), all your personal files (photos, video clips, songs, Word and Excel files etc.) will not be lost. You can easily do this with the help of a free software called Partition Wizard (or to give its full name MiniTool Partition Wizard). Here are step-by-step instructions on how to go about doing this.

item.title: How to use TeamViewer to transfer files
item.description: At some point in our cyber life we would have to buy a new computer. What then? Are we going to forgo all those nice folders containing precious files (photos, music, video clips, etc.) that have been built up lovingly over the years and which are in the old PC? Certainly not , if only we know how to transfer all that stuff to our new PC. This is where TeamViewer comes in.

item.title: Your 2016 Calendar
item.description: Your 2016 calendar is ready for printing. Go here: http://pgoh13.com/2016_calendar.php

item.title: Songs And Lyrics - a website for those who like to sing with the lyrics.
item.description: Songs just added: I Really Like You (Carly Rae Jepsen ft. Tom Hanks), Somebody (Natalie La Rose ft. Jeremih), Something in the Water (Carrie Underwood), Give Me Everything (Pitbull ft. Ne-Yo, Afrojack, Nayer), Time Of Our Lives (Pitbull ft. Ne-Yo), I Want You To Know (Zedd ft. Selena Gomez), Hold Back The River (James Bay), Heartbeat Song (Kelly Clarkson), GDFR (Goin' Down For Real) by Flo Rida ft. Sage The Gemini and Lookas, King (Years & Years), Real (Years & Years), Trouble (Iggy Azalea ft. Jennifer Hudson), When The Beat Drops Out (Marlon Roudette)

item.title: Songs And Lyrics - a website for those who like to sing with the lyrics.
item.description:
Songs just added: Haunted (Beyonce), If It's Magic (Stevie Wonder), Drunk In Love (Beyonce ft. Jay Z), Fancy (Iggy Azalea), Bang Bang (Jessie J. ft. Ariana Grande, Nicki Minaj), Restart (Sam Smith), New Flame (Chris Brown ft. Usher & Rick Ross), Spotlight (Jennifer Hudson), Good Kisser (Usher), My Heart Is Open (Maroon 5 ft. Gwen Stefani), Somethin' Bad (Miranda Lambert ft. Carrie Underwood), The House That Built Me (Miranda Lambert), Little Red Wagon (Miranda Lambert), Hold My Hand (Brandy Clark), Heart Is A Drum (Beck), A Feather's Not A Bird (Rosanne Cash), Story Of My Life (One Direction), It's Your World (Jennifer Hudson ft. R. Kelly), Heart Attack (Demi Lovato), I Love It (Icona Pop ft. Charli XCX)
(Songs added on 7 Feb. 2015) Say Something (Karen Harding), Jealous (Nick Jonas), L.A. Love (Fergie ft. YG), Gravity (DJ Fresh ft. Ella Eyre), CoCo (O.T. Genasis), The Hanging Tree (James Newton Howard ft. Jennifer Lawrence), Elastic Heart (Sia ft. Shia LaBeouf & Maddie Ziegler), Salted Wound (Sia in "Fifty Shades Of Grey"), Love Me Like You Do (Ellie Goulding), FourFiveSeconds (Rihanna ft Kanye West, Paul McCartney), Only Human (Cheryl), Right There (Nicole Scherzinger ft. 50 Cent), Buttons (The Pussycat Dolls ft. Snoop Dogg), Lay Me Down (Sam Smith)
Doing It (Charli XCX ft.Rita Ora), Coming With You (Ne-Yo), Sugar (Maroon 5), The Nights (Avicii), If You Wait (London Grammar), Strong (London Grammar), Wasting My Young Years (London Grammar), I'm Not The Only One (Sam Smith), Hold You Down (DJ Khaled), Habits (Tove Lo), Heroes we could be (Alesso ft. Tove Lo), All About That Bass (Meghan Trainor), Take me to church (Hozier), Up (Olly Murs ft. Demi Lovato), Let it go (Demi Lovato), Blank Space (Taylor Swift), Something I need (OneRepublic), Lips Are Moving (Meghan Trainor), These days (Take That), Uptown Funk (Mark Ronson ft. Bruno Mars), Thinking out loud (Ed Sheeran), Steal my girl (One Direction), I don't care (Cheryl), Prayer In C (Lilly Wood and the Prick), Am I wrong (Nico & Vinz), Ghost (Ella Henderson), Love runs out (OneRepublic), Anything could happen (Ellie Goulding),Maps (Maroon 5), Stay with me (Sam Smith), Black widow (Iggy Azalea ft. Rita Ora), Lovers on the sun (David Guetta ft. Sam Martin), Rude (MAGIC!), Shake it off (Taylor Swift), Just the two of us (Bill Withers), This is the life (Amy Macdonald), Rhythm of my heart (Rod Stewart), Problem (Ariana Grande), All of me (John Legend) and Titanium (Sia).
Songs added earlier:
I will never let you down (Rita Ora), Waves (Mr Probz), Sing (Ed Sheeran), Lay me down (Avicii), Summer (Calvin Harris), The man (Aloe Blacc), Lua (Bright Eyes), A sky full of stars (Coldplay), Stolen dance (Milky Chance), You and me (Disclosure ft. Eliza Doolittle), Rise like a phoenix (Conchita Wurst), Don't give up (Peter Gabriel ft. Kate Bush), Forever love (Gary Barlow) (25 April 2014) Milkshake (Kelis), Money on my mind (Sam Smith), Addicted to you (Avicii), Dark horse (Katy Perry), Magic (Coldplay), Enjoy the silence (Depeche Mode), Rather be (Clean Bandit ft. Jess Glynne), Hey brother (Avicii), Can't remember to forget you (Shakira ft. Rihanna), The monster (Eminem ft. Rihanna), Royals (Lorde), Timber (Pitbull ft. Ke$ha), Counting Stars (OneRepublic), (12 March) California king bed (Rihanna), Hall of fame (The Script ft. Will.i.am), Budapest (George Ezra), Some nights (Fun), Carry on (Fun), Bonfire heart (James Blunt), Happy (Pharrell Williams), Black horse and the cherry tree (KT Tunstall), You give me something (James Morrison), You will never know (Imany), I'm yours (Jason Mraz), Let her go (Passenger), (31 January 2014) Fire (Ayo ft. Youssoupha), I'm walking (Ayo), Sweeter than fiction (Taylor Swift), Waterfalls (TLC), Say something (A Great Big World & Christina Aguilera), Little drummer boy (Pentatonix), Ready or not (Bridgit Mendler), Fare thee well/Dink's Song (Oscar Isaac & Marcus Mumford), Honey you don't know my mind (Jimmy Martin), Blue moon of Kentucky (Bill Monroe), Man of constant sorrow (Ralph Stanley), Walk on the wild side (Lou Reed), We r who we r (Kesha), Love me again (John Newman), Home sweet home (Motley Crue), Started from the bottom (Drake), The cave (Mumford and Sons), Little lion man (Mumford and Sons), We can't stop (Miley Cyrus), Wrecking ball (Miley Cyrus), One love (Blue), It's a man's world (Christina Aguilera), On my knees (Jaci Velasquez), Your man (Josh Turner), Anything goes (Randy Houser), I could be the one (Avicii), Wake me up (Avicii), Riverside (Agnes Obel), Safe and sound (Capital Cities), Set fire to the rain (Adele), Behind blue eyes (The Who), The A Team (Ed Sheeran), Thank you (Dikta), Good days and bad days (PJ Morton), Instant crush (Daft Punk ft. Julian Casablancas), Can't hold us (Macklemore & Ryan Lewis ft. Ray Dalton), Blurred Lines (Robin Thicke), Locked out of Heaven (Bruno Mars).
Hear the song. Sing with the lyrics. This website is meant for people who enjoy singing but who don't often remember the lyrics.

item.title: Free and useful software to download and install.
item.description:
(20 January 2015) If your new PC or your present one has only one main drive (C:) you might want to consider creating another drive to put all your personal files there so that in case your PC breaks down and Drive C: has to be reformatted in order to reinstall Windows (or any other operating system), all your personal files (photos, video clips, songs, Word and Excel files etc.) will not be lost.
PrivaZer is a very powerful program that is able to empty your Recycle Bin completely so that it doesn't leave a trace.
A jewel! This "Print Edit" addon to the Firefox browser is really cool. It will allow you to print only what you want from an internet page. Why should you waste ink printing out all the advertisements or navigational bars or even pictures? Click on above heading for more.
If you have had the frustrating experience of clicking on a video clip only to be met with the words "This video is not available in your country" or if you'd gone to netflix.com hoping to watch movies at home only to read a message saying "Sorry, Netflix hasn't come to this part of the world yet", you'd still be able to see them thanks to hola.org...
WizTree is a good program to help you find files or folders that you didn't succeed in finding on your computer. I discovered it while looking for a program to help me look for a missing folder which I was positive was on my hard disk.
Would you believe it if I were to tell you that anybody, yes even you, can make a flip-over postcard like this? (Click here to see demo). You can easily replace the holiday photo and text to suit yourself. To do this go to the Webmaker website.
If you want to try out new programs without installing them, go to http://spoon.net. Never heard of spoon.net? Well, you don't know what you are missing, especially the less temerarious among us who dare not install new programs because we are afraid that this would result in all kinds of problems to our computer as a result. In fact it is as if you have all those programs on your desktop, the only difference being that they are virtual, that is, you can make use of them but they're not on your computer itself thus giving you zero danger. Go to spoon.net, open an account with them and you'll have a better idea what they have to offer.
There is a free program called Wise Folder Hider which will be of interest to those who would like to hide their personal folders or files (by which we also mean private photos, videos or sensitive information) from people sharing their computer. You can download it from here (click on Direct Download Link). Once installed, you can hide a file or a folder simply by right clicking on it and choosing the "Hide File/Folder with Wise Folder Hider" option. This will work even if the program is not open. But of course you will have to open the program (protected with the password that you gave it) if you want to look at those files yourself.

item.title: English to Malay Dictionary by pgoh13
item.description: The English to Malay Dictionary by pgoh13 has been updated today. Many more new words and English idiomatic expressions have been included, the latest being "excoriate".

item.title: Using the Ctrl button for shortcuts
item.description: The Ctrl button on the keyboard is a great time-saver as it can be used for all kinds of shortcuts with a letter of the alphabet. If you have not been using it you might want to find out how it can make your surfing and working life easier on your computer. More here

item.title: Visiting Milan in Italy
item.description:
The capital of Lombardy and the largest Italian city after Rome, the flourishing city of Milan (or Milano, its local name) will soon have the world at its doorsteps for six months, as the World Expo 2015 (Universal Exposition) will be held there from Friday 01 May to Saturday 31 October 2015.
The following top European cities have already been covered: Lisbon, Dublin, Sicily, Amsterdam, Athens, Barcelona, Cannes, Copenhagen, Geneva, London, Madrid, Paris, Prague, Rome, Venice, Budapest, Sevilla etc.

item.title: Evergreens and Oldies
item.description: If you long to listen to those evergreens and oldies of the fifties, sixties, seventies or eighties (Elvis Presley, Connie Francis, Beatles, Pat Boone, Bob Dylan, Joan Baez, Paul Anka, etc. etc.) here's all of them in one page.

item.title: Basic Malay Language Course by pgoh13
item.description:
Lesson 13 has been updated today. Lesson 58 on comparisons has also been updated following a query by a student who wants to know how to make "negative" comparisons instead of "positive" ones.
(19 August 2014) I have updated Lesson 6 today. Read explanation on when to use "ramai" and when to use "banyak".
(04 August 2014) I have added a page on Malay verbs that use the -i suffix here. There are lots of illustrative sentences to help you use it correctly though this is one area where the Malay language is definitely more difficult than other languages.
(17 January 2014) Lesson 29 has been updated to include a full list of the commonest members of the family. Apart from the linguistic aspect, there is also the cultural aspect to be kept in mind when you are talking about your siblings in Malay. This is because while in English we can just say "He is my brother" or "She is my sister" and leave it at that (that is, without revealing if he or she is older or younger than yourself), this is simply not possible in Malay where you have to specify each time if it is your younger or elder brother or sister you are talking about
(16 November 2013) Today I have revised Lesson 38 on the use of "Tolong", the word to use when you are shouting for help or asking a service from someone.
In response to a student's email I have added a page of explanations and illustrative sentences on the various uses of the Malay word pun. Go here to learn about the various meanings of the Malay word pun.
In case you have forgotten, the verb "to be" is often left out in Malay. So when you ask the questions below you only have to say: "Where washroom?, Where my shoes?, Where Petaling Street?, Where he?, Where my key?" Don't laugh - after all Malay is not English!

item.title: Closing of sub-domain http://malay.pgoh13.com
item.description: The sub-domain http://malay.pgoh13.com has been closed. You can access the Basic Malaysian and Indonesian Language Course of 64 lessons from the main domain at http://pgoh13.com. Go to the top navigation bar, put your mouse on "Languages" then from the dropdown menu click on "Malaysian and Indonesian languages".

item.title: Computer and Internet Tips
item.description:
Are you sick and tired of seeing certain programs (eg. Real Player, Skype etc.) that you do not need, start automatically each and every time you start your computer? To make sure you don't see them in future...

As you know, IE (Internet Explorer) and FF (Firefox) are not indispensable for internet. There are other standard browsers like Chrome and Safari with Opera making some little headway. But if you are using a small netbook you might want to use a really "lightweight" browser such as Maxthon. As a matter of fact it is advisable to have at least two browsers on your computer so that if a video clip or song doesn't open, you can always try your luck with the other browser..... More here.

Do you find yourself having to send a dozen photos or even some 50 (god forbid!) photos by email? The best way to do this (if you are using Windows) is to.....

Are you tired of having to go to the Start button every time you want to shut down your computer? Why not create a shortcut on your desktop for this so that you can shut down your PC very easily...

If you want to print an email message from your Gmail account without having to print the column on the left (containing Inbox, Sent Mail, etc) nor the sponsored links/advertisements on the right you have to click on New window (not from File - New Window but "New Window" on the right-side column of your email) and then click on "Print all" from there.

During your internet session you might have to wait a very long time for a particular site to open. You don't mind waiting as it is an important site to you. But should you keep on waiting forever? After all the webhosting server of the website might be down and no amount of waiting will do you any good. On the other hand the problem could be with your computer or with the ISP that you subscribe to and you could be able to access it from another computer. But how would you know? There is one way to find out.

item.title: Layman's Guide To Diseases
item.description: You have all heard of HIV but that is only one of the better-known medical abbreviations often used in diagnostics. What if you hear someone is suffering from ALS, AMD, BPH, BPD, CVA, DVT, ED, GERD, HPV, IBD, IBS, LIS, ME, MND, MS, MSA, OSA, RA, SARC, SLE, STD, TIA or UTI? Go here for a quick rundown of the disease.

item.title: Claypot chicken rice in rice cooker
item.description: Yearning for claypot chicken rice but don't have a gas cooker so you can't use a claypot? Well, try cooking the dish in a rice cooker instead. This is how I did it.

item.title: Quotations and ideas that inspire
item.description:
Just added this citation by T.D. Jakes: When people can walk away from you, let them walk. I don't want you to try to talk another person into staying with you, loving you, calling you, caring about you, coming to see you. Your destiny is never tied to anybody that left.....Stop begging people to stay. Let them go!! If someone can't treat you right, love you back, and see your worth. Let it go!!!

Among other quotations in this page:
When you plant lettuce, if it does not grow well, you don't blame the lettuce. You look for reasons it is not doing well. It may need fertilizer, or more water, or less sun. You never blame the lettuce. Yet if we have problems with our friends or family, we blame the other person. But if we know how to take care of them, they will grow well, like the lettuce. (Thich Nhat Hanh, a Vietnamese Zen Buddhist monk)
You must take personal responsibility for your life. You cannot change the circumstances, the seasons, or the wind, but you can change yourself. (Jim Rohn)
Don't cry because it's over. Smile because it happened. (Dr Seuss)
The ultimate measure of a man is not where he stands in moments of comfort and convenience, but where he stands at times of challenge and controversy. (Martin Luther King Jr.)

Click on above heading for similar quotations like the above. This is the place to go if you are down and need something to pep you up! You will begin to see things in a different light.

item.title: What's new in Paris.
item.description: The Picasso Museum in Paris will finally re-open on 25 October 2014 after being closed for renovation works that extended to three years. Paris Plage, a month-long yearly event along the banks of the Seine River as well as at Bassin de la Villette (near the Stalingrad underground station) will be held from 19 July 2104 to 17 August. Its sandy "beaches", numerous activities and free deckchairs will make you feel that you're on holiday. A great place to bring the kids!

item.title: Deezer Playlists: Listen to today's top 20 songs nonstop!
item.description: I have just added a playlist of the following songs: I will never let you down (Rita Ora), Waves (Mr Probz), Sing (Ed Sheeran), Lay me down (Avicii), Down to the river to pray (Alison Krauss), Summer (Calvin Harris), The man (Aloe Blacc), Lua (Bright Eyes), A sky full of stars (Coldplay), Stolen dance (Milky Chance), You and me (Disclosure), Don't give up (Peter Gabriel), Forever love (Gary Barlow), Everybody hurts (R.E.M.), Milkshake (Kelis), Money on my mind (Sam Smith), Addicted to you (Avicii), Dark horse (Katy Perry), Magic (Coldplay) and Enjoy the silence (Depeche Mode). You can find the link here: http://pgoh13.com/deezer/

item.title: Differences between the Malaysian and Indonesian languages
item.description: This will be of interest to students of the Malay language course in this website. Although the course is based on Malay as spoken in Malaysia yet it is quite similar to the Indonesian language. But what are the main differences? Go here for a list of the some of the words that you need to use depending on whether you are in Malaysia or in Indonesia.

item.title: Slow and sentimental songs non-stop
item.description: If you love slow and sentimental songs, you'll enjoy this playlist of 40 songs which will play non-stop for over 2 hours. Among the song titles in the list are: To know him is to love him, Yesterday when I was young, If you go away, My one and only love, It's alright with me, You don't know me, You do something to me, But not for me.....

item.title: Listen to Eurovision 2014's winning song
item.description: Listen to Eurovision 2014's winning song "Rise like a phoenix" by Conchita Wurst here. Lyrics provided.

item.title: The 2015 Calendar is now available
item.description: For those of you who like to plan well ahead: here's the 2015 Calendar with dates of important events in Year 2015.

item.title: Country domain initials used in Internet
item.description:
I've just added a page on "Country domain initials used in Internet" (ccTLD or "country code top-level domain") here: http://pgoh13.com/country_domains.php
It could be useful to you one day when you come across a URL or email address having a strange-looking two-letter initial to know where it comes from.

item.title: The greatest French songs of all time (Les plus belles chansons francaises de tous les temps)
item.description:
The following French oldies have been added:
Il n'y a plus d'après (Juliette Gréco), Les couleurs du temps (Guy Béart), Le Sud (Nino Ferrer), Tous les bateaux, tous les oiseaux (Michel Polnareff), J'ai encore revé d'elle (Il était une fois), Quelques mots d'amour (Michel Berger), Etretat (Pierre Bachelet), Ménilmontant (Charles Trenet), Padam Padam (Edith Piaf), La java bleue (Fréhel), Un gamin de Paris (Yves Montand), She (Charles Aznavour) and La romance de Paris (Charles Trenet).
This is my personal collection of what I consider to be the greatest French songs of all time. There are of course the usual standards by legendary figures like Charles Trenet, Edith Piaf, Juliette Greco, Jacques Brel, Serge Gainsbourg, George Brassens, Francoise Hardy, Barbara and a few others. You will be able to listen and sing along with the lyrics in French that accompany each song.

item.title: A Page For Senior Citizens
item.description: This page has been added today. If you are a senior citizen, AOP (Age Old Pensioner) or retiree you will surely find this page interesting.

item.title: Popular Chinese restaurants in Paris
item.description: While finding Chinese restaurants is the easiest thing to do in Paris (there is at least one in every street) yet finding one that serves good, authentic Chinese cuisine but which is not too pricey at the same time is no mean task. Here are a few restaurants that offer not only great food but also down-to-earth prices for them.

item.title: Health and Food Sections
item.description:
I have introduced a HEALTH section to the website. This page has articles on common health issues and topics as well as a list of top health sites that answer all your questions concerning health issues and diseases.
I have also introduced a FOOD section. This page handpicks recipes for 10 popular global dishes and has a list of top cooking sites where you should be able to find the recipe you are looking for. You can go to these two pages from the top navigational menu bar in every page.

item.title: 2013 Calendar of Events
item.description: A calendar for Year 2013 has just been added with all the important dates for you to note in your agenda.

item.title: Another popular Chinese song added to "Popular Chinese songs of all times"
item.description: Another all-time Chinese hit, called Minjiang Nocturne, sung by a young talent Miss Cai Xing Yuan, has been added to "Popular Chinese songs of all times". Enjoy.

item.title: Announcement
item.description: Due to a server problem, this site was down for 3 1/2 hours this morning. Sorry about that. It is up and running again now. If in future you cannot access this site you will be able to know if it is due to a webhosting server problem or if it is just your own network provider or computer that has a problem by typing pgoh13.com in the search box of this site: http://www.checksite.us/ or this one http://downorblocked.net/ Click on it now to have an idea of what you will see. You will have to note this url somewhere else (the best place is probably under Bookmarks) as you will not be able to access this page in order to click on it if this website is down. Makes sense, doesn't it?

item.title: Listen to Deezer playlists while you work
item.description: LATEST ADDITIONS: Listen non-stop to playlist of songs from Deezer. Latest additions: Gotye, Joni Mitchell, Jessie J, Nicki Minaj, Queen and Fiona Apple. Also: Grammy award winners Adele and Foo Fighters as well as songs by Luna Del Rey and Amel Larrieux. Enjoy! Go here to listen to the various playlists.

item.title: How to create a strong yet easy-to-remember password
item.description: One good way to create a solid password is to use the first letters of the words from a sentence that you find easy to remember, because it is something that is meaningful to you and you alone eg. you can easily come up with a strong password like: iwf4lyiP which is made up of the first letters of the eight words in this meaningful sentence: I worked for 4 long years in Paris

item.title: Internet Scams and Email Spams
item.description: I have updated this page today. The article discusses email spams and internet scams in all its various aspects and prepares you for what you might be up against.

item.title: The World At Your Fingertips
item.description: I have added the automatic Google machine translator to allow instantaneous translation of my pages into some 50 languages. You will find it right at the top left-hand corner of every page (under "Select Language"). As you know, machine translations can be hilarious at times, especially when they concern idiomatic expressions, but nevertheless they still do give the gist of what a page is all about.

item.title: Old Songs New Singers
item.description: I have just added "Bridge Over Troubled Water" sung by Sissel and Russell Watson as well as the 1973 song "Desperado" by the Eagles, here sung by Melanie Amaro, winner of the U.S. X-Factor on 22 Dec. 2011. Enjoy!

item.title: RssReader for the layman or How to make use of a news aggregator.
item.description: You must have seen this orange icon in many websites. While it is possible to read the page by just clicking on it this is not the whole idea behind it. It is meant to be part of your collection of similar feeds from many other different websites that are of interest to you to be put together in a news reader or feed aggregator to save you the trouble of having to visit each site in turn. This page will show you how to go about installing a rss news aggregator.

item.title: Visiting Paris - Go shopping by boat in Paris
item.description: If you have run out of ideas on where to go or what to do in Paris how about taking a boat ride to go shopping? More here about Le Millenaire shopping mall.

item.title: Is it possible to write sheet music with just abc?
item.description: If there is any music student or aspiring musician among you here, you will be glad to hear about this free programme that allows you to write your own sheet music by just typing text like "abc" or "do re mi". The programme is called LilyPond and it does this by encoding the musical notes from letters and numbers. Even a non-musician like me is able to make use of it. After all the principal notes are represented by the letters "c d e f g a b". Look at my feeble attempt in using it here.

item.title: Homemade ginger drink
item.description: If you have had enough of drinking coffee or tea, here's a drink that you can easily prepare for yourself. It can even help to soothe your sore throat and to make your cold less of an agony (as you know once you have caught a cold you just have to bear with it and let it take its course). To make the drink you need the following three items: a lemon, ginger and honey.

item.title: Malay to English Dictionary by pgoh13
item.description:
The "Malay to English Dictionary by pgoh13" has been updated. Click to go.
But if you prefer to use the bilingual English-Malay and Malay-English dictionary then go here.

item.title: Easy access to Google Maps for address
item.description: I have put a box in the left navigational menu of every page for you to type an address anywhere in the world in order to find its exact location with Google Maps. No more worries about how to get to your friend's house, your hotel or your place of interview! This page explains how to use it for showing you the route to take for going from A to B.

item.title: Useful Tips for YouTube Users
item.description: Go to this page to find out how to listen to a YouTube song over and over again or how to copy a song from a YouTube video clip to play in your mp3 player or how to jump to the middle of a YouTube clip. And from here you will also be taken to the most-watched video clips of the moment.

item.title: New features to make the site even more useful
item.description:
I have added "Talking Point" to the home page of the website as well as "Anything to translate" from Google where you can paste a text, a document file or the URL of an internet page and have it translated from any language to another of the over 50 languages available. This is in addition to the following:
A link to get the exact time of the country you are interested in at this very moment;
A link to get current weather conditions of the city you are interested in; and
A box for you to type any address in the world to find out its exact location.
This site is really living up to its title "The World At Your Fingertips".

item.title: List of health topics for quick reference
item.description: When you hear that a man is suffering from ED what does it mean? Go here to learn more about this (and many other health topics and medical diseases).

item.title: Backing up your files automatically
item.description: You know how important it is to have a backup of all your files in case your computer suddenly conks out one day and you are not able to access anything in it. You can do this either with a USB memory key or an external hard disk. You can even have this done for you every day without your having ever to think about it again. Go here to learn how.

item.title: Visiting Malaysia
item.description:
Hi,
I have just finished my pages on Kuala Lumpur, Penang, Malacca, Kota Bharu, Cameron Highlands and the Perhentian islands. Hope they will be useful to those planning a trip to Malaysia.

item.title: The World's My Home
item.description: This page is so useful you might need to come back to it often! I have replaced the links for "World Public Holidays" and "World Embassies" with better ones. You know that you can find all the information you need about anything at all in internet - provided you know how to go about finding that information. Of course there is Google. But in today's age where everyone has someone out there somewhere in some part of the world there are certain types of information that we need to have at our fingertips (or rather, within the reach of your mouse) at any time of the day or night and here is where I hope this page will serve its purpose.

item.title: New Sitemap For The World At Your Fingertips
item.description: I have just redone the Sitemap to include some 15 "obsolete pages" (so-called because they will not be updated) but which still serve their purpose.

item.title: Learn to speak Mandarin through Pinyin
item.description:
This course bends some of the rules in order to make it easier to study Pinyin, which is the romanised phonetic version of Mandarin. Email received from a student:
Hi,
I just want to thank you for the very clear and simple Pinyin lesson. I wish I have found it 6 years earlier when I just started learning Mandarin. It would have saved me from a confusing discussion with a taxi driver in Beijing on the difference between "Huatang" and "Huadang".
It is interesting to note that under the Wade-Giles system of romanization, no distinction is made between j and zh, as it represents both by ch (voiceless). Similarly it makes no distinction between q and ch, representing both by ch' (voiced). From this it can be seen that even for a big part of the Chinese themselves, what are represented as two different sounds under pinyin is actually the same sound. Again I ask, why be pedantic and see differences where the Chinese themselves do not?

item.title: Jokes to brighten up the day.
item.description:
A girl invites her boyfriend to come over for dinner with her parents. And tells him that after dinner, she would "do it" with him for the first time. Never having done this before, the boy goes to the local pharmacist for advice...

Click on link for this joke and many more.
